summary: Iter2: tested community library + admin moderation feature. Backend 18/18 pytest PASSED (role on auth, PUT public_status pending, /community/cards shows only approved + owner_name, /admin/pending-cards 403 for non-admin + 401 unauth, approve/reject happy-path + 404 for non-pending, clone of approved card by non-owner succeeds with new id + public_status=private, owner self-clone 400, clone-non-approved 404, reject flow). Frontend verified end-to-end after fixing 2 blocker bugs introduced by main agent.

ui_bugs.issue: File had duplicate dangling JSX (lines 60-66) causing webpack SyntaxError 'Unexpected token' — entire frontend failed to compile. ALSO /comunidade and /admin/moderacao routes were missing from <Routes> even though CommunityPage and AdminModerationPage were imported.

setUser({id,email,name}) was missing the role field returned by /auth/login and /auth/register, so admin-only 'Moderação' sidebar link (gated by user?.role === 'admin') did NOT appear immediately after login — only after a full page refresh when GET /auth/me repopulated role.

action_items: Both frontend blockers were FIXED by testing agent (App.js routes + auth.jsx role propagation). Main agent should review the fixes and keep them.
(Carry-over from iter1) CORS allow_origins='*' with allow_credentials=True is spec-invalid; no brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py clone endpoint correctly scopes to public_status='approved', assigns new id/user_id, resets public_status='private' — solid isolation.
require_admin helper is clean; 403 vs 401 behavior correct (unauth returns 401 via get_current_user, authed non-admin returns 403).
approve/reject only match documents with public_status='pending' — prevents re-approving already-approved/rejected cards (verified by test).
Layout.jsx role-gating is pure client-side — server-side /admin/* endpoints correctly enforce 403 regardless (defence in depth OK).
CardBuilderPage has UX: 'Comunidade' section only appears for saved cards (requires id) — reasonable but not tested via UI in this iter.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/App.js (removed dangling duplicate JSX + added /comunidade and /admin/moderacao routes)
/app/frontend/src/lib/auth.jsx (login/register now set role on user state)
test_credentials: admin@geekcards.com / admin123 (seeded, role=admin). Additional users registered during test with TEST_/UITEST_ prefix; cleaned up by test.
seed_data_creation: A few UITEST_* cards may persist in test user libraries; test users themselves remain (registration is one-way via API). Harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Community library feature fully working after iter2 fixes. Regression files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py + /app/backend/tests/test_community.py. Run both with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Frontend key flows: admin login -> Moderação link visible, /comunidade lists approved + owner_name + Copy button, regular user sees no Moderação, non-owner Copy clones card to private library. CardBuilder 'Solicitar publicação' button (data-testid='card-submit-public-btn') was not UI-tested end-to-end in iter2 (API path was) — main agent may verify manually.
rca of the issue: Two pre-existing bugs in main agent's iter2 implementation: (1) App.js had duplicate JSX block appended, crashing webpack; root cause likely a botched copy/paste during route addition. Fix: rewrote App.js cleanly and added the two missing <Route> entries for /comunidade and /admin/moderacao. (2) auth.jsx did not propagate the role returned by /auth/login|register into local user state, so role-based UI gating only worked after a hard refresh. Fix: include data.role in setUser(...) for both login and register.
